title: Enable Steam on M1 via asahi gaming branch
description:
Enable Steam on the M1 MacBook by switching the asahi channel to the 'gaming' branch and wiring FEX-Emu + muvm + asahi-steam into the home/system Guix configs. Stack: FEX-Emu binfmt (x86/x86_64), muvm microVM runtime, asahi-steam launcher (wraps Alyssa Rosenzweig's shim.py), Mesa overlay packages for GPU passthrough.

## Files touched
- modules/r0man/guix/channels.scm (pin asahi-channel to gaming branch + commit)
- modules/r0man/guix/system/m1.scm (drop qemu-x86_64 binfmt, splice %asahi-gaming-services, enable FEX + steam)
- modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/m1.scm (splice %asahi-home-gaming-services for PipeWire bridge)

## References (do not edit; upstream asahi-guix gaming branch)
- ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules/asahi/guix/services/gaming.scm
- ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules/asahi/guix/services/fex-binfmt.scm
- ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules/asahi/guix/home/services/gaming.scm
- ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules/asahi/guix/home/services/sound.scm

## Key structural facts
- Gaming aggregator is split: %asahi-gaming-services (system) vs %asahi-home-gaming-services (home).
- %qemu-service-aarch64 in this repo registers qemu-x86_64 binfmt → collides with FEX-x86_64. Must drop.
- Mesa overlay wiring is automatic via asahi-fex-binfmt-configuration's overlays field + activation-service-type symlinking .erofs files into /usr/share/fex-emu/overlays/.
- Config.json has NO Overlays key — verify symlinks, not JSON.
- asahi-substitutes serves only 'main'; first pull after switching to gaming rebuilds fex-emu, muvm, overlays, rootfs-fedora from source (long).

Plan: /home/roman/.claude/plans/polymorphic-fluttering-noodle.md

title: Create asahi-linux skill
description: Build a ~/.claude/skills/asahi-linux/ skill covering (a) running/troubleshooting Fedora Asahi Remix on M1 and (b) hacking on the upstream Asahi stack (m1n1, installer, mesa, kernel, FEX, speakersafetyd, scripts). Mine local checkouts in ~/workspace/ and the AsahiLinux GitHub org for latest info. Explicitly NOT about asahi-guix (separate skill later).
close_reason: Skill committed to main (a69bfbf). Iteration-1 benchmark: 100% pass rate with skill vs 48.6% baseline across 3 test cases (boot recovery, Steam setup, M3 GPU dev). Gaming rebased on top of main.

title: End-to-end verification: binfmt, overlays, WirePlumber drop-in, Steam launch
description:
Run all verification steps in order. Do not skip past a failure.

## 1. Binfmt sanity
  cat /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/FEX-x86_64  # enabled, interpreter /gnu/store/...-muvm/bin/muvm
  cat /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/FEX-x86
  ls /proc/sys/fs/binfmt_misc/ | grep -i qemu   # expect: NO match

## 2. Env, Config, overlay symlinks
  echo "$FEX_APP_DATA_LOCATION"                     # /etc/fex-emu
  test -f /etc/fex-emu/Config.json && jq . /etc/fex-emu/Config.json
  ls -l /usr/share/fex-emu/overlays/*.erofs         # two symlinks into /gnu/store/...-mesa-fex-emu-overlay-*
  which steam muvm FEXInterpreter
(Note: Config.json has no 'Overlays' key — symlinks are the source of truth.)

## 3. WirePlumber drop-in landed
  test -L ~/.config/wireplumber/scripts/client/access-muvm.lua && \
    readlink ~/.config/wireplumber/scripts/client/access-muvm.lua
  # Expect: path ending in .../muvm/share/wireplumber/scripts/client/access-muvm.lua
  herd status wireplumber   # running

## 4. Launch Steam
  steam

First launch shows PyQt6 splash while shim.py downloads Steam bootstrap. Runtime checks:
- Steam's in-client system info shows Asahi/honeykrisp GPU, NOT llvmpipe. If llvmpipe → re-check overlay symlinks in step 2.
- Audio + microphone work in-game → confirms home-level bridge is live.
- Controllers visible in Big Picture → confirms %udev-hidraw-service loaded and re-login happened.

## Rollback (if needed)
- guix system roll-back
- guix home roll-back
- Restore channels.scm to (branch "main") then guix pull
- BEFORE guix gc: sudo herd restart binfmt. FEX binfmt entries use the 'F' (fix-binary) flag which pins an open FD to the store-path 'muvm' of the rolled-back generation. If GC collects that path before binfmt re-registration, x86 execve silently breaks.

## Done when
All 4 steps pass; Steam renders with honeykrisp; audio + controllers work.

title: Reconfigure system + home, restart pipewire/wireplumber, re-login
description:
Apply the edits to a live M1 generation.

## 1. guix pull (long first time — no substitutes on gaming)
After Task A's channels.scm is committed and Tasks C+D are in place:
  guix pull

## 2. Reconfigure system
Use sudo -E so the pulled guix remains on PATH:
  sudo -E guix system -L modules reconfigure \
       modules/r0man/guix/system/m1.scm

## 3. Reconfigure home
  guix home -L modules reconfigure \
       modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/m1.scm

## 4. Restart audio services
WirePlumber does NOT hot-reload conf.d; the new access-muvm.lua drop-in only takes effect after restart:
  herd restart pipewire
  herd restart wireplumber

## 5. Re-login
Session-level env vars (FEX_APP_DATA_LOCATION, PATH additions) only apply to new sessions. Binfmt POCF entries are global — no re-login needed for those.

## Done when
System + home generations switched; 'herd status' shows pipewire/wireplumber running; user has logged back in.

title: Edit home/environments/m1.scm: splice %asahi-home-gaming-services (muvm PipeWire bridge)
description:
Wire the home-level PipeWire bridge (WirePlumber drop-in) from the gaming home aggregator into the M1 home environment.

## 1. Add import to modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/m1.scm
#:use-module (asahi guix home services gaming)     ; %asahi-home-gaming-services

## 2. Splice into services list
In the existing (define services (append home-tmux-services (list …))):

(define services
  (append home-tmux-services
          %asahi-home-gaming-services
          (list …existing services…)))

## Effect
Extends home-pipewire-service-type (already active here) via its drop-ins extension point, overlaying muvm's WirePlumber config (access-muvm.lua). Guests running under FEX+muvm can reach the host PipeWire graph.

## Security note (from bridge docstring in (asahi guix home services sound))
Enabling the bridge grants every muvm guest process full access to all host capture devices unless a portal-permissionstore is running to mediate. Voice chat / in-game mics need it.

## Done when
Diff shows import added + %asahi-home-gaming-services spliced; 'guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/m1.scm' succeeds.

title: Edit system/m1.scm: drop qemu-aarch64, add gaming imports, splice %asahi-gaming-services with FEX enabled
description:
Three coordinated edits to modules/r0man/guix/system/m1.scm.

## 1. Add imports
#:use-module (asahi guix services gaming)          ; %asahi-gaming-services
#:use-module (asahi guix services fex-binfmt)      ; asahi-fex-binfmt-configuration

## 2. Resolve binfmt collision with %qemu-service-aarch64
%qemu-service-aarch64 (defined in modules/r0man/guix/system/services/qemu.scm) registers qemu-x86_64 binfmt handlers which collide with FEX's FEX-x86_64. Non-deterministic winner across rebuilds.

Action: DROP %qemu-service-aarch64 from the cons* at ~line 139 of system/m1.scm. Leave qemu.scm untouched — %qemu-service-x86-64 (Precision) is unaffected.

No qemu-* binfmt should remain; qemu-binfmt-service-type is not pulled in transitively by desktop-operating-system.

## 3. Splice %asahi-gaming-services with FEX + Steam enabled
Use modify-services to flip asahi-fex-binfmt-configuration's (disabled? #t) default to (disabled? #f)(steam? #t):

(define %gaming-services
  (modify-services %asahi-gaming-services
    (asahi-fex-binfmt-service-type cfg =>
      (asahi-fex-binfmt-configuration
       (inherit cfg)
       (disabled? #f)
       (steam? #t)))))

Splice %gaming-services INSIDE the existing outer modify-services's first argument (not outside the wrapper):

(modify-services (append %gaming-services
                         (cons* (service iwd-service-type)
                                …existing…
                                (operating-system-user-services desktop-operating-system)))
  …unchanged delete / guix-service-type clauses…)

## Effect
Brings in muvm, fex-emu, fex-emu-rootfs-fedora, fex-emu-thunks, asahi-steam, two mesa-fex-emu-overlay-* packages via fex-profile-entries. Renders /etc/fex-emu/Config.json, creates /usr/share/fex-emu/overlays/*.erofs symlinks, exports FEX_APP_DATA_LOCATION=/etc/fex-emu. %udev-hidraw-service tags /dev/hidraw* with uaccess for controllers.

## Mesa overlays — no action needed
Default value of overlays field already supplies both mesa-fex-emu-overlay-{x86-64,i386}; activation-service-type extension creates the symlinks. Leave field at default.

## Done when
Diff of system/m1.scm shows two new imports, %qemu-service-aarch64 removed from cons*, %gaming-services defined and appended. Smoke test (task B) passes.

title: Pre-pull smoke test: guix system build with -L worktree
description:
Run a local evaluation-only build against the asahi-guix gaming worktree BEFORE running 'guix pull'. Catches eval errors (e.g., home-pipewire-in-system-context) without touching the channel.

## Command
guix system -L modules \
            -L ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules \
            build modules/r0man/guix/system/m1.scm

## Scope
- Smoke test only. DO NOT use -L ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming/modules for 'guix system reconfigure' — the worktree ships modules with the same paths as the installed channel (binfmt.scm, sound.scm, substitutes.scm, udev.scm, firmware.scm); -L shadows them wholesale and 'guix system's %fresh-auto-compile #t triggers the module-recompile footgun (see project memory).
- This task assumes Task C (system/m1.scm edits) has been drafted locally so there is something meaningful to build. If smoke-testing an in-progress branch, run this as you iterate on system/m1.scm.

## Done when
'guix system build' exits 0 for m1.scm with both -L paths.

title: Pin asahi-channel to gaming branch + commit SHA
description:
Edit modules/r0man/guix/channels.scm to switch asahi-channel from main to the gaming branch with a commit-pin.

## Change
In (define-public asahi-channel ...):
- Set (branch "gaming")
- Add (commit "<HEAD sha of asahi-guix gaming branch>") alongside the branch field
- Keep url https://codeberg.org/asahi-guix/channel and introduction unchanged

## How to get the SHA
cd ~/workspace/asahi-guix/channel/gaming && git rev-parse HEAD
(Confirm this commit includes fex-overlay-activation in modules/asahi/guix/services/fex-binfmt.scm — the overlays field + activation-service-type wiring. Without it Steam falls back to llvmpipe.)

## Why commit-pin not just branch
Prevents silent drift from 'guix pull' and keeps home environment pinned to a known state.

## Caveat (communicate to user)
asahi-substitutes-service-type serves only 'main' — first 'guix pull' after switch will rebuild fex-emu, muvm, mesa-fex-emu-overlay-*, and fex-emu-rootfs-fedora from source on the M1. Expect one long build.

## Done when
channels.scm diff shows (branch "gaming") and (commit "<sha>") in asahi-channel; file parses with 'guix repl'.

title: Dry-run verification for home and system configs
description:
Final verification step. Run each dry-run command below and confirm clean success before closing the epic.

## Dry-run home environments

```
guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/m1.scm
guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/precision.scm
guix home -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/home/environments/server.scm
```

## Dry-run system

```
guix system -L modules --dry-run reconfigure modules/r0man/guix/system/m1.scm
```

## REPL sanity check

```
guix repl -L modules
,use (r0man guix home services fzf)
,use (r0man guix system services openssh)
,use (r0man guix system services keyboard)
```

## Grep sweeps for stragglers

```
grep -rn "(r0man guix home \(btop\|claude-code\|clojure\|...\))" modules/
grep -rn "(r0man guix system \(services\|keyboard\|xorg\))" modules/
```

The greps should return no matches against the old module paths for the 24 moved home modules, and no references to `(r0man guix system services)` (the bare form), `(r0man guix system keyboard)`, or `(r0man guix system xorg)` anywhere in `modules/`.
close_reason: m1 home dry-run: clean. server home dry-run: clean. m1 system dry-run: clean (the two unbound-variable warnings without file:line are benign per memory). precision home dry-run fails on pre-existing 'beads-next does not support aarch64-linux' issue — verified by stashing refactor and reproducing same error. REPL module loads for home/services/{fzf,claude-code,emacs} + system/services/{openssh,keyboard,xorg,slim} all succeed. Grep sweeps confirm no stragglers referencing old paths.

title: Delete system/services.scm and update system callers
description:
Steps 5 and 6 of the mechanical procedure. Delete the now-redundant monolithic `modules/r0man/guix/system/services.scm` and update the three system config callers with the specific per-service imports they need.

**`system/base.scm`** — currently uses `%libvirt-service`, `%openssh-service`, `%keyboard-layout`, `console-font-service-config`:
```diff
- #:use-module (r0man guix system keyboard)
- #:use-module (r0man guix system services)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services console-font)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services keyboard)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services libvirt)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services openssh)
```

**`system/desktop.scm`** — currently uses `%auditd-service-type`, `%bluetooth-service`, `%containerd-service`, `%cups-service`, `%libvirt-service`, `%nix-service`, `%openssh-service`, `%pcscd-service`, `%slim-service`, `%udev-fido2-service`, `console-font-service-config`:
```diff
- #:use-module (r0man guix system services)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services auditd)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services bluetooth)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services console-font)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services containerd)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services cups)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services libvirt)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services nix)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services openssh)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services pcscd)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services slim)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services udev-fido2)
```

**`system/m1.scm`** — currently uses `%keyboard-layout`, `%xorg-libinput-config`, `%xorg-modeset-config`, `%qemu-service-aarch64`:
```diff
- #:use-module (r0man guix system keyboard)
- #:use-module (r0man guix system services)
- #:use-module (r0man guix system xorg)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services keyboard)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services qemu)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix system services xorg)
```

**Not changed:** `system/m1-test.scm` only imports `(r0man guix system m1)`. `deploy/m1.scm` and `.guix/manifest.scm` only import `(r0man guix system m1)` and `(r0man guix home environments m1)`.

Finally, delete `modules/r0man/guix/system/services.scm`.

title: Split system/services.scm into 19 per-service files
description:
Step 4 of the mechanical procedure. Create 19 new files under `modules/r0man/guix/system/services/<name>.scm`, each containing one logical unit copied verbatim from the monolithic `system/services.scm`. Each new file gets module header `(r0man guix system services <name>)` and only the `gnu`/`guix` imports it actually needs.

| New file | Exports | Source lines in old file |
|---|---|---|
| `system/services/auditd.scm` | `%auditd-service-type` | 50–51 |
| `system/services/avahi.scm` | `%avahi-service` | 53–54 |
| `system/services/bluetooth.scm` | `%bluetooth-service` | 56–57 |
| `system/services/cups.scm` | `%cups-service` | 59–64 |
| `system/services/containerd.scm` | `%containerd-service` | 66–67 |
| `system/services/docker.scm` | `%docker-service` | 69–70 |
| `system/services/elogind.scm` | `%elogind-service` | 72–73 |
| `system/services/guix-publish.scm` | `%guix-publish-service` | 75–79 |
| `system/services/certbot.scm` | `certbot-ssl-certificate`, `certbot-ssl-certificate-key` | 81–85 |
| `system/services/libvirt.scm` | `%libvirt-service` | 87–92 |
| `system/services/nix.scm` | `%nix-service` | 94–95 |
| `system/services/openssh.scm` | `%openssh-service` | 97–103 |
| `system/services/pcscd.scm` | `%pcscd-service` | 105–106 |
| `system/services/postgresql.scm` | `%postgresql-service` | 108–111 |
| `system/services/qemu.scm` | `%qemu-service-aarch64`, `%qemu-service-x86-64` | 113–121 |
| `system/services/slim.scm` | `%slim-service` | 123–129 |
| `system/services/screen-locker.scm` | `%screen-locker-service` | 131–135 |
| `system/services/udev-fido2.scm` | `%udev-fido2-service` | 137–138 |
| `system/services/console-font.scm` | `console-font-service-config` | 140–143 |

**Special case:** `slim.scm` additionally imports `(r0man guix system services keyboard)` and `(r0man guix system services xorg)` (the helpers moved in the previous task).

Do not yet delete `system/services.scm` — that happens in the next task after the callers are updated.

title: Move system/keyboard.scm and system/xorg.scm under system/services/
description:
Step 3 of the mechanical procedure. `git mv` the two config-data helper files under `system/services/` and rewrite their `define-module` headers.

| Current path | New path | Module header |
|---|---|---|
| `system/keyboard.scm` | `system/services/keyboard.scm` | `(r0man guix system services keyboard)` |
| `system/xorg.scm` | `system/services/xorg.scm` | `(r0man guix system services xorg)` |

These are config-data helpers that don't define service types, but per the user's decision they live under `services/` anyway so that the split-out `slim.scm` (see next task) can cleanly import both as `(r0man guix system services keyboard)` and `(r0man guix system services xorg)`.

Not touched: `system/base.scm`, `system/desktop.scm`, `system/m1.scm`, `system/m1-test.scm` (OS configurations). Already correct: `system/services/substitutes.scm`.

title: Rewrite home environment imports for moved modules
description:
Step 2 of the mechanical procedure. Sweep the 5 `home/environments/*.scm` files and rewrite imports for the 24 moved modules from `(r0man guix home <name>)` to `(r0man guix home services <name>)`.

Example diff:
```diff
- #:use-module (r0man guix home btop)
+ #:use-module (r0man guix home services btop)
```

Files to edit:
- `home/environments/m1.scm` — ~22 rewrites
- `home/environments/precision.scm` — ~24 rewrites
- `home/environments/server.scm` — ~13 rewrites
- `home/environments/gastown.scm` — ~2 rewrites (emacs, environment)
- `home/environments/container.scm` — ~7 rewrites

**ONLY rewrite imports for the 24 moved modules.** Imports for helpers — `bash`, `channels`, `desktop`, `gpg`, `msmtp`, `packages`, `pm`, `sound`, `ssh`, `tmux`, `xdg` — must remain untouched at `(r0man guix home <name>)`.

Note: `home/services/gastown.scm` already imports `(r0man guix home environments container)` — unchanged.

title: Reorganize home/system service modules under services/
description:
## Context

Currently most home service modules live directly under `modules/r0man/guix/home/*.scm` (e.g. `(r0man guix home fzf)`), mixed in with helpers and environment configs. Two files have already adopted the new layout (`home/services/gastown.scm`, `system/services/substitutes.scm`), showing the direction: reusable services grouped under a `services/` subdirectory.

**Goal:** Move all strict home service-type modules to `(r0man guix home services <name>)` and split the monolithic `system/services.scm` into one file per service under `(r0man guix system services <name>)`. Pure rename/relocation refactor — no behavioral changes.

## Scope

**Part A (Home side):** Move 24 home service files from `home/<name>.scm` to `home/services/<name>.scm`, rewriting module headers to `(r0man guix home services <name>)`. Sweep the 5 `home/environments/*.scm` caller files and rewrite imports for the 24 moved modules only. Helper/wrapper modules (bash, channels, desktop, gpg, msmtp, packages, pm, sound, ssh, tmux, xdg) stay put.

**Part B (System side):** Split monolithic `system/services.scm` into 19 per-service files under `system/services/<name>.scm`, each with module header `(r0man guix system services <name>)`. Also move `system/keyboard.scm` and `system/xorg.scm` under `system/services/`. Update callers in `system/base.scm`, `system/desktop.scm`, `system/m1.scm` with specific per-service imports. Delete the old `system/services.scm`.

## Files touched at a glance

- Moved (26): 24 home service files + `system/keyboard.scm` + `system/xorg.scm`
- Created (19): split files under `system/services/`
- Deleted (1): `system/services.scm`
- Edited headers (26): all moved files get new `define-module`
- Edited imports (8): `home/environments/{m1,precision,server,gastown,container}.scm` + `system/{base,desktop,m1}.scm`
close_reason: All 6 children complete. Home side: 24 service modules moved to home/services/, all environment imports rewritten. System side: keyboard.scm and xorg.scm moved under system/services/; monolithic system/services.scm split into 19 per-service files and deleted; callers updated. Extra: relative local-file paths fixed (files/ -> ../files/) for moved home service modules, and precision.scm home environment's system/keyboard+xorg imports updated.
